findings.evidence: `../mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/HopfAlgebra/TensorProduct.lean:73` has the general `TensorProduct.antipode_def : antipode S (A := B ⊗[R] A) = AlgebraTensorModule.map (antipode S) (antipode R)`, while the PR proves only the `K ⊗[k] A` specialization by `simp [TensorProduct.antipode_def]`.
findings.fix: First add the general pure-tensor lemma, e.g. `TensorProduct.antipode_tmul : antipode S (A := B ⊗[R] A) (b ⊗ₜ[R] a) = antipode S b ⊗ₜ[R] antipode R a`, and derive the base-change statement from it.
findings.issue: `HopfAlgebra.baseChange_antipode_tmul` is a special case of the natural tensor-product Hopf algebra formula, with the left factor specialized to the base ring `K`.

summary: The new base-change API is mostly at the intended point level, but the antipode pure-tensor lemma is stated only for the base-change special case even though Mathlib already exposes the fully general tensor-product antipode definition.
